draft.introduction: Romans 8:1–17 declares freedom from condemnation for those in Christ and introduces the life-giving work of the Spirit. It is Paul’s most sustained treatment of the Holy Spirit’s role in the believer’s transformation.

translation.text: There is therefore now no condemnation for those who are in Christ Jesus. For the law of the Spirit of life has set you free in Christ Jesus from the law of sin and death.
translation.original_text: οὐδὲν ἄρα νῦν κατάκριμα τοῖς ἐν Χριστῷ Ἰησοῦ

translator_notes.note: Sinaiticus omits the later addition “who walk not according to the flesh but according to the Spirit” found in the Textus Receptus. The shorter reading makes the declaration of “no condemnation” more absolute and unconditional.
